channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Property groups caution that reducing capital gains tax discount could harm housing supply
item.description: Australia's major property groups have expressed concern to the federal government over plans to reduce the capital gains tax (CGT) discount. The current policy offers a 50 per cent discount on profit...

item.title: Government Finalises Plan to Build 6,800 Houses for Prison Officers Across Tanzania
item.description: The Tanzanian government has finalised a comprehensive plan to construct 6,800 houses for prison officers across the country as part of efforts to address long-standing accommodation shortages within ...

item.title: Canadian Markets Open with Caution Amid Earnings and Middle East Updates
item.description: Global and Canadian markets opened cautiously following yesterday's gains, as investors digested a mix of corporate earnings reports and monitored developments in the Middle East. TSX futures edged lo...

item.title: Oyegun Talk Say ADC Ready to Challenge Tinubu for 2027 Presidential Election
item.description: Former APC chairman, Chief John Odigie-Oyegun, don talk say African Democratic Congress (ADC) dey fully ready to contest for power come 2027 presidential election and take charge for Aso Rock. He yarn...

item.title: Canadian investment sector seeks delay in CRA’s GST changes on trailer commissions
item.description: Canadian wealth management and investment industry groups are urging the federal government to delay the implementation of new GST/HST rules on mutual fund trailing commissions. The Canada Revenue Age...

item.title: EFF Leader Julius Malema to Be Sentenced Over 2018 Firearm Incident
item.description: Julius Malema, leader of the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF), is due to be sentenced today at the KuGompo City Magistrates Court following his conviction for breaching firearm laws. The incident dates...

item.title: Philippines Advocates Regional Cooperation on Oil Stockpiling Amid Energy Crisis
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. has urged for a regional study on joint oil stockpiling to address the energy disruptions triggered by the ongoing Middle East crisis. Speaking at the Asia Ze...

item.title: ABS-CBN Responds to Pension Concerns Amidst Internal Disputes
item.description: ABS-CBN is facing renewed scrutiny and defending its pension plan amidst an ongoing family dispute within the Lopez family, the media conglomerate's owners. The company has publicly stated that the pe...

item.title: Hong Kong Secondary School Selected for First Time to Join NBA Rising Stars Invitational in Singapore
item.description: A secondary school from Hong Kong has been invited to participate in the NBA Rising Stars Invitational for the first time, marking a significant milestone for basketball in the region. The yet-to-be-n...

item.title: Trump criticises Starmer over UK stance on Iran conflict and hints at trade deal changes
item.description: US President Donald Trump has escalated tensions with the UK government by threatening to alter the trade agreement signed last year with Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The dispute centres on Starmer's ...

item.title: Families of Apartheid Victims Continue Struggle for Justice Decades Later
item.description: The article explores the enduring struggle of South African families seeking justice for atrocities committed during apartheid, focusing on the infamous case of the Cradock Four. On 27 June 1985, Fort...

item.title: Philippine-flagged Ships Allowed Safe Passage Through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Contrary to circulating social media claims, Philippine-flagged vessels are indeed passing through the Strait of Hormuz. The Department of Energy confirmed that following a safe passage agreement with...

item.title: South African fuel prices projected to rise significantly in May amid global instability
item.description: South African motorists should prepare for notable increases in fuel prices in May, as the Central Energy Fund (CEF) reports growing under-recoveries across all fuel grades. The current international ...

item.title: Pakistan Stock Exchange surges with optimism over US-Iran diplomatic talks
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) saw a strong rally on Wednesday, with the KSE-100 index rising by 2,885.10 points, or 1.74%, closing at 168,519.94 points. This bullish trend continued from Tuesday’s...

item.title: Prime Minister Modi advocates nine citizen resolutions for national progress at Adichunchanagiri event
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, while addressing a large gathering at Adichunchanagiri Mutt in Mandya district near Mysuru on April 15, 2026, urged Indians to come together and embrace a set of nine beh...

item.title: How Prediction Markets Reflect the Modern Obsession With Forecasting
item.description: In the article 'Oil Up or Down? The Inexorable Rise of Predictions Markets,' Moss and Fog explores how the explosion of information — largely driven by smartphones and social media — has deepened pola...

item.title: Windsor Spits Take Key Step Toward Western Conference Championship with Flint Win
item.description: The Windsor Spits took a significant step toward securing the Western Conference championship following their recent victory over the Flint Firebirds. The win marked a crucial moment in the Spits' pla...

item.title: Lacson pauses Senate inquiry on flood control issues to finalize partial report
item.description: Senate Blue Ribbon Committee chairperson Senator Panfilo Lacson has temporarily suspended further hearings on alleged anomalies in flood control projects until the panel submits and sponsors a partial...

item.title: Simeone praise Lookman for his key role in Atlético’s Champions League success
item.description: Diego Simeone, the head coach of Atlético Madrid, don hail Nigerian forward Ademola Lookman say him be one kind special player wey Atlético never get before. The Argentine talk this one after Lookman ...

item.title: China‑built spy satellite allegedly used by Iran to monitor US military bases in Middle East
item.description: A report by the Financial Times, cited in this article, claims that Iran used a Chinese‑built military‑grade spy satellite called TEE‑01B to monitor key US military bases across the Middle East during...
